title: CBP Agents Kill Alex Pretti, Second U.S. Citizen Fatally Shot in Minneapolis
excerpt: Border Patrol agents shot and killed Alex Pretti, a 37-year-old ICU nurse and VA employee, on a Minneapolis street during Operation Metro Surge—the second fatal shooting of a U.S. citizen by federal immigration agents in the city within three weeks. Bystander videos contradicted the DHS account that Pretti had 'brandished' a weapon, showing him holding only a phone before being tackled and shot.
content:
On January 24, 2026, two U.S. Customs and Border Protection officers fatally shot Alex Jeffrey Pretti, a 37-year-old ICU nurse at the Minneapolis VA hospital, on a south Minneapolis street during an immigration enforcement operation. The killing was the second fatal shooting of a U.S. citizen by federal agents in Minneapolis within three weeks, following the January 7 death of Renee Good.

  • DHS Secretary Kristi Noem immediately described Pretti's actions as 'domestic terrorism,' claiming he 'brandished' a weapon and 'attacked' officers.
  • Bystander video reviewed by Reuters, the BBC, the Wall Street Journal, and the Associated Press appeared to show Pretti holding only a cellphone; bystander video showed an agent removing his legally-carried firearm from his holster before shots were fired.
  • A CBP internal preliminary report forwarded to Congress stated officers said Pretti 'resisted arrest' but made no mention of him attacking officers or threatening them with a weapon, contradicting the White House's narrative.
  • Federal agents physically blocked state Bureau of Criminal Apprehension investigators from accessing the crime scene.
  • Ten shots were fired. ProPublica subsequently identified the two agents: Border Patrol Agent Jesus Ochoa and CBP Officer Raymundo Gutierrez. Both remained on administrative duty and were not removed from the agency.
  • The Hennepin County Medical Examiner ruled Pretti's death a homicide on February 2, 2026.
'How many more residents, how many more Americans need to die or get badly hurt for this operation to end?' — Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey
mediaPlaceholder: Bystander video of Pretti being tackled and shot by federal agents on a Minneapolis street
